Narrative_Meaning: Sixes signify harmony and adaptation, but they can also indicate hard work or unexpected challenges.

Yarn.Yarn_Description: The Card of light, warmth and hope. But one should also be aware that light can blind, warmth can burn and hope can be false. While everything about this card is direct and to the point, just as the sun appears to move straight across the sky, this is not the actual truth and things appear to change, as we see them in different lights.

Significator.Plot: The Sun Plot is about Hope. It offers hope to the hopeless, a new hope to the downtrodden, and reversals of fortunes. Of course, the truth is that for every hope that the Sun grants, another must be dashed. For every plant that grows in the light, another is scorched and dies of dehydration, because it would have preferred the shade.
Significator.Side: The Side that has the Sun on their side has been marked as being powerful and important. They are on the rise, growing in power, and are not tested as strongly by the Plot as other sides. However, if they do fail, failure will be especially harsh to them, and may see Deaths and Destruction.
Significator.Character:
The Sun Significator indicates a Character who Gains Yin when they are warm and pleasant.

Gains Yang when they are blunt or harsh.

They Gain Chi when they give people hope or inspire them.

They also gain +1 Success Level when they are stood in sunshine.
Significator.Diegetic_Character: A radiant and charismatic figure, capable of both gentle warmth and blinding intensity. They are a natural source of inspiration for others, moving through the world with a directness that can either illuminate or scorch depending on their mood.
Significator.Scene: The Sun Scenes are generally outdoors, often fun involving sun, sea and sand at the beach, but occasionally tortuous, sun, sand and no water to see, desert scenes. The sand is completely optional. Sun Scenes are usually Ordeals, but may be Tests with a Gain or Revelations, and are often just rests (or Wefts). Yarn-Tellers can decide if the Sun is harmful (sun burn, sun stroke and heat exhaustion) or helpful (restful, fun, pleasant weather) during play.

Yarn.Aspect_Description: The Hook targets the Character's Geometry Yin Gain, whatever is required to gain Yin will be offered by the Hook.
Yarn.Hook_Description: The story hooks the character with a morning messenger (or other media such as the morning newspaper or update) bearing important news. It may be news that a friend is ill, or that the enemy is closing in, or that you have won a lottery. Regardless, this begins the Conflict with a Gain or Revelation.
Yarn.Diegetic_Gain: A wealth of gold and precious stones, gleaming with a light that promises prosperity and a higher station in life.
Yarn.Fray_Description: A High Stakes Solitary Motional Ordeal, where you must survive a dangerous situation, travel and often accomplish other tasks along the way, it might be as simple as making it back to the oasis after your camel was bitten by a snake, or it could be dashing through rush hour traffic to deliver pizzas when a shoot-out takes place. There are usually Obstacles involved and occasionally Traps.
Yarn.Snag_Description: Although the Characters may have achieved whatever was required, it is no longer enough, or they misunderstood what they were looking at. Changes to orders, Rule changes, Late entries, Disqualifications, Reinforcements, or even moving the goalposts on the Pressed side can all be caused by Changing Light. Often the changing light is the fault of the Dominant Embodiment, other times it just happens. The Snag may act as a Revelation, but what exactly was revealed by the Changing Light?

NPC.Description: The Nerd has some subject, genre, or technology that obsesses them. There's nothing they love more than to talk about or consume their "books / shows".

Yarn.Sweeping_Text: The Pressed side experiences the warmth and glory of the Sun, and gets to bask in it. The Basking Character usually makes a Gain as they are rewarded, and appreciated for what happened in the Fray.
Yarn.History_Description: The Sun indicates here that in the past there was a time and place of great joy and hope.
Yarn.Situation_Description: The Sun here indicates a good situation, this is usually not a situation that you want to change, you want to enjoy it. Of course, such a pleasant situation will never last.
Yarn.Solution_Description: The Sun tells us that no matter how dark things seem right now (and they do seem bad) this will pass. The light will always return, the sun will always rise again.

Revelations.About_Description: The information is about the background, or setting, not the current plot or situation. It might include pertinent details for the current plot, but is usually about the morality of the world. Is this world a dark place, or is it filled with hope? Is there are balance between the forces of good and evil, or has one side overwhelmed the other, or has some covenant, pact or treaty been signed that guarantees a few sparks of light in the darkness?
Revelations.Info_Description: Information about how far someone may go to achieve their Ends. Will they kill, or die for it? Or will they not even get out of bed in the morning?
Revelations.Vector_Description: The information reaches the characters by images and images alone, this usually means in the form of static photographs (or portraits or sketches), but this might include video files, visions (whether spiritual, psychic or psycho-pharmaceutical in origin, seeing something at great distance or seeing only the shadows cast by the actions behind a curtain.
Revelations.Alternate_Description: The information is about the Successes or accomplishments of a Character, Pact, or Descendant.
Revelations.Detail_Description: Fine details include minutia about the Character etc. these might include one of the ways they Gain Yin / Yang, or which brand of Laser gun they prefer.

Obstacle.Description: The way is blocked, by bright lights that could ruin night and low-light vision.

Tarot.Crossed: Target describes a Scene from their past. Allows purchase of Proficiencies, Descendants, etc.
Tarot.Normal: Player may describe a Scene from the Target's past. Usually that shows some previous connection to the Player Character.

Ordeal.Stage_Description: A medium length, curved path, street or road, a slightly odd situation or place.
Ordeal.Motional_Description: Reach and complete the goal Stage as quickly as possible, usually riding a jet-bike, eagle or something similar.
Ordeal.Tide_of_Battle_Text: The Flowing and Ebbing foes are drawn apart by the battle. Causing a lull or pause between assaults for both sides. Either side can use this moment to heal, or stabilize a Wound, or to take stock of the battle, and decide where to lend your strength (Prepare an Action in your Preferred Style).
Ordeal.Fight_Description:
A group of PC level opponents, or a single Monster with at least one Monster Facet and a Boon equal to the Value equal to at least three quarters of the total Value of all PCs Personality Annexes
  Facet    Monster  
Awe ⚊ ♦

Suit: Diamonds ♦

Ordeal Trump: Preparation (Research / Work / Patience) / Movement (Sneaking / Sprinting) / Listening

Description: Diamonds represent the finer things of life, they are bright and sparkling. Representing the creative even spiritual aspects of materialism such as beauty and art, that transcend base materialism like wealth. Despite this the Diamonds are recognised as being the Coins suit of the Tarot, with a strong tie to Elemental Earth.

Tarot Meaning: Travel, Money, Business, Contracts, Deals, Voyages.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Travel (e.g. Chase, Race) / Creativity (e.g. Smithing, Painting). Diamonds act as Trump for the following Action Types: Art, Building, Charming, Crafting, Driving, Emoting, Forgiving, Keeping Your Cool, Listening, Meditation, Motion, Prayer, Preparation, Research, Sprinting and Working in general. Any type of action can be prepared with a Diamond.

Parry:Seize the initiativeThe cards of the original attack (less the matched card) are immediately added to the Defender’s Ordeal Pool. They may then play as many cards as their Descendant/Annex normally plays including the Parry card (1-4) usually into one of their Style Reserve, and then immediately discard any cards over Pool Limit.

Soak: ReadyThe matched Attack card is immediately Prepared by the Defender (as they wish). The Soak card is discarded.

Terror — The Monster is a Fear or Anxiety embodied. They are quite diverse in form, but are always able to cause Psych attacks and Negative Psych effects to any that see them act. They may add Awe Draw cards to their Monster Pool whenever they cause negative emotions in another Character. They automatically draw and play Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) cards as a Psych Attack on any Character that sees them make any attack. They must take Awe Draw cards from their Monster Pool to defend, Soak or Heal any Attack made upon them. Examples of this Monster include Ghosts, some serial killers and some Preternatural Cosmic Horrors.
Craft ⚊ ♦

Suit: Diamonds ♦

Ordeal Trump: Preparation (Research / Work / Patience) / Movement (Sneaking / Sprinting) / Listening

Description: Diamonds represent the finer things of life, they are bright and sparkling. Representing the creative even spiritual aspects of materialism such as beauty and art, that transcend base materialism like wealth. Despite this the Diamonds are recognised as being the Coins suit of the Tarot, with a strong tie to Elemental Earth.

Any type of action can be prepared with a Diamond.

Parry:Seize the initiativeThe cards of the original attack (less the matched card) are immediately added to the Defender’s Ordeal Pool. They may then play as many cards as their Descendant/Annex normally plays including the Parry card (1-4) usually into one of their Style Reserve, and then immediately discard any cards over Pool Limit.

Soak: ReadyThe matched Attack card is immediately Prepared by the Defender (as they wish). The Soak card is discarded.

Maker — The Monster is actually a creator of other Monsters. The spawn are often less powerful than the creator, but in some cases are unique and more powerful. The Creator may not be directly encountered until late in the Story. The Creator can draw Craft Draw cards and add them to its Monster Pool each time it creates or summons an Extra or Descendant. At least 1 card must be used from the Monster Pool each Summoning or Creation to help pay the Summoning costs (or as an Obstacle / NPC Yarn card). All currently summoned Extras and Descendants receive a bonus to their Annex Scores and card Pips equal to the Creator’s Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced).
Orthodox ⚋ ♦

Suit: Diamonds ♦

Ordeal Trump: Preparation (Research / Work / Patience) / Movement (Sneaking / Sprinting) / Listening

Description: Diamonds represent the finer things of life, they are bright and sparkling. Representing the creative even spiritual aspects of materialism such as beauty and art, that transcend base materialism like wealth. Despite this the Diamonds are recognised as being the Coins suit of the Tarot, with a strong tie to Elemental Earth.

They may then play as many cards as their Descendant/Annex normally plays including the Parry card (1-4) usually into one of their Style Reserve, and then immediately discard any cards over Pool Limit.

Soak: ReadyThe matched Attack card is immediately Prepared by the Defender (as they wish). The Soak card is discarded.

Tyrant — The Orthodox Monster is a tyrant, often a part of “the system” (in that it acts as a method of control over a population). A Tyrant monster add Orthodox Draw cards to their Monster Pool every time they are cruel, ruthless, authoritarian or demanding. Tyrants can add Monster Pool Size Reduced Success Levels on another Character’s actions if they order them to do something. Tyrants can also add Monster Pool Size Score Orthodox Failure Levels to an enemy action for a cost of one card from their Monster Pool. The Tyrant must discard half of their their Monster Pool if they are ignored, forgotten or scorned.
Quiet ⚋ ♦

Suit: Diamonds ♦

Ordeal Trump: Preparation (Research / Work / Patience) / Movement (Sneaking / Sprinting) / Listening

Description: Diamonds represent the finer things of life, they are bright and sparkling. Soak: ReadyThe matched Attack card is immediately Prepared by the Defender (as they wish). The Soak card is discarded.

Hush — The Quiet Monster is exactly that, almost (or entirely) silent. It is the creeping death of a hidden illness, or the cat-footed predator striking without warning that vanishes without trace. Hush Monsters get to begin with Quiet Score cards in their Monster Pool. Hushes may add +1 Score / Pip for each card in their Monster Pool when they try to act silently. When a Hush creature makes a noise (often a scream or howl, including a Psych attack), it must play at least half of the Monster Pool on the action. Hush Monsters may add cards to their Monster Pool based on how long it has been since the last time they made a noise as a Yarn Extend Duration (e.g. After being silent for a few seconds add a card, after a scene of silence add two. Be silent for a week and add 6 cards to the Pool). Creatures like giant owls, snakes or cats, that stalk and hunt silently are good examples, as is a really creepy, floating mime. Many ghosts are also silent, until they turn and scream to create a Duck attack.
Virtue ⚋ ♦

Suit: Diamonds ♦

Ordeal Trump: Preparation (Research / Work / Patience) / Movement (Sneaking / Sprinting) / Listening

Description: Diamonds represent the finer things of life, they are bright and sparkling. Soak: ReadyThe matched Attack card is immediately Prepared by the Defender (as they wish). The Soak card is discarded.

Friendly — The Monster is a “Friendly” monster, that will almost certainly be misunderstood and attacked by some people. They may add Virtue Draw cards to their Monster Pool by being kind to another, or whenever they are attacked. A friendly Monster protects their friends from harm, and must play cards from their Monster Pool to defend, protect, or heal them. Friendly monsters may add a number of Failure Levels equal to the Monster Pool Size Reduced to any enemy that tries to target them or their friends. Friendly Monsters can add Virtue Score (rolled Die or otherwise) to any positive action when playing a card from their Monster Pool. Examples might include a cynical, but big-hearted ogre; a being created from stitched together corpses; a dark-elf ranger with a unicorn necklace; or perhaps even an Electric Mouse.
Zeal ⚊ ♦

Suit: Diamonds ♦

Ordeal Trump: Preparation (Research / Work / Patience) / Movement (Sneaking / Sprinting) / Listening

Description: Diamonds represent the finer things of life, they are bright and sparkling. Representing the creative even spiritual aspects of materialism such as beauty and art, that transcend base materialism like wealth. Soak: ReadyThe matched Attack card is immediately Prepared by the Defender (as they wish). The Soak card is discarded.

Celestial — The Monster is a Celestial, a creature of divine or supernatural nature, often associated with a particular divinity, occasionally a political association, they often act as heralds of greater powers. Celestial beings include Anthropomorphic Personifications of social constructs like, Death, War, and Wyrd herself. When acting according to their nature, following their belief system, performing their duties, or following orders, they may draw Zeal Draw Ordeal cards into their Monster Pool. They automatically add Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) Success Levels to any action to do with their nature, duty, travel, or communication. They also add Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) to actions specific to their nature and duty. Celestial Monsters can only manifest a physical incarnation by playing cards directly from their Monster Pool on all physical actions. The monster could be an angel, a devil, a djinn, or perhaps even one of the Four Horsemen of the Apocalypse.
  Facet    Monster

Lea.Example: Mice / Hedgehog / Squirrel / "Small" Fish and most Birds including Pigeons

Stress.Description: Stress and its results can do odd things to a person. Sometimes it can drive them wild, other times it can "save" them, driving them into the loving arms of religion. Usually this will be a new religion for the Character, perhaps a new church or pastor if their old religion, but something about what they are selling the Character really seems to be buying. This can represent a spiritual awakening, or a vulnerable sort being preyed on by a cult.
Stress.Rules: Characters are offered a choice. They may take a new Hitch (Emotional, Inept, Bias, Mild, Duty or Devotion) with Banes equal to the the Character’s current Stress, or they may increase another Hitch by 1 Bane for Stress equal to the current Hitch Banes, or they may discard parts of their Character such as Proficiencies, Descendants, Allies or even Annexes which will negate Stress equal to the Cost of the lost part in Chi.

Trauma.Description: Trauma and Shock can be powerful forces on a Character’s mind and behaviour, but minds can be resilient even in the face of odds that others may call hopeless, and fo some individuals hope will remain.
Trauma.Rules: The Trauma converts all current Stress to Hopes (Liberty Facet Sway), additional Hopes may be converted into other Yang Facet Sway for storage.

Age.Description: An Age of Enlightenment is occurring. During this age great discoveries are made, it is an age of exploration, both geographically and culturally. The society looks beyond itself, and seeks betterment by incorporation of ideas it finds there. These ideas may come from the society's past, a neighbouring culture or group (as during the actual enlightenment), some distant colonised land, or as a result of some out-group within the society (such as mathematicians, scientists, clergy or magicians). A society will often evolve culturally and technologically during this age, and may not resemble itself at all following the age.
Age.Diegetic_Description: A time of transformation and discovery, where society looks beyond its borders to find new truths. It is an era of cultural and geographical exploration, where the influx of new ideas from distant lands or ancient pasts reshapes every aspect of daily life and thought.
Age.Rules: Enlightenment is pictured as shining a light upon oneself and one's culture, but this must usually be done from an exterior position, such as through the window of a foreign or ancient culture. Elightenment can have a profound affect upon a society, changing almost everything about its culture in a revolutionary way.
